TeoNAM STAM — OLS GWAS on the imputed map panel (Fig 4C)

Per-marker across-family OLS on the FSFHap-imputed map-panel genotypes — computation, model & QC

Author

Fausto Rodríguez-Zapata

Published

July 5, 2026

What this is. The computation behind the STAM OLS GWAS scan on the imputed map panel (data/teonam/stam_gwas_scan_unimputed.csv), tracked in its own right. It ports the throwaway agent/teonam_stam_gwas_unimputed.R into a rendered notebook so the scan — not just its Manhattan — is reproducible from primary data. The annotated Fig-4C Manhattan that consumes this CSV lives in teonam-qtl-recovery; this notebook produces it.

The genotypes are FSFHap-imputed (teonam_map_v5_gwas, 51,004 markers — Chen’s map construction, complete within family). Each marker is tested only on the families that typed it (cross-family gaps left as NA, not densified), giving the natural GWAS scatter with no step-interpolation plateau artifact. (The plain-unimputed in the CSV filename means “not cross-family-densified”, not “raw genotypes”.)

library(here)
library(data.table)
library(parallel)
library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
theme_set(theme_bw(base_size = 12))
THREADS <- max(1L, detectCores() - 2L)
LOD <- 5 # Chen 2019 significance line: P < 1e-5

Inputs (all primary data — no .rds intermediates)

# FSFHap-imputed recoded dosages (complete within family): 1237 lines x 51,482 markers; NA = cross-family untyped (0/1/2 = W22 / het / teosinte)
geno <- fread(here("data/teonam/TeoNAM_genotype_clean.csv"))

# robust key = family + line, e.g. "TIL01" + "A001"; family from the population column
key <- paste0(sub("^W22", "", geno[[2]]), sub("^.*Line_", "", geno[[1]]))
fam <- factor(sub("^W22", "", geno[[2]]))

# phenotype: STAM (staminate spikelet), joined by line key
ph <- as.data.frame(read_excel(
  here("data/teonam/9250682/TeoNAM_1257RILs_22traits_phenotype_data.xlsx")
))
names(ph)[1] <- "line"
y <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(setNames(ph$STAM, ph$line)[key]))

# v5 coordinates + consensus cM; restrict the scan to markers that lifted to v5
mc <- fread(here("data/teonam/marker_info_v5_cm.tsv"))
chr_by <- setNames(mc$chr_v5, mc$marker)
pos_by <- setNames(mc$pos_v5, mc$marker)
mk <- intersect(names(geno)[-(1:3)], mc$marker)

data.table(
  quantity = c(
    "lines", "markers (raw)", "markers (lifted, scanned)",
    "STAM phenotyped", "families"
  ),
  value = c(
    nrow(geno), ncol(geno) - 3L, length(mk),
    sum(!is.na(y)), nlevels(fam)
  )
)
                    quantity value
                      <char> <int>
1:                     lines  1237
2:             markers (raw) 51482
3: markers (lifted, scanned) 51004
4:           STAM phenotyped  1236
5:                  families     5

The model

For each marker \(m\), restrict to the lines \(i\) with a non-missing dosage \(g_{im}\in\{0,1,2\}\) and a non-missing STAM value \(y_i\), then compare two nested least-squares fits:

\[ \textbf{Null:}\quad y_i = \mu + \sum_{f} \beta_f \,\mathbb{1}[\text{family}_i = f] + \varepsilon_i \] \[ \textbf{Full:}\quad y_i = \mu + \sum_{f} \beta_f \,\mathbb{1}[\text{family}_i = f] + \gamma\, g_{im} + \varepsilon_i \]

The marker enters as a single additive dosage effect \(\gamma\) shared across families (1 numerator df). Significance is the marginal \(F\)-test of \(\gamma\):

\[ F = \frac{(\mathrm{RSS}_0 - \mathrm{RSS}_1)/1}{\mathrm{RSS}_1/(n - \mathrm{rank})}, \qquad P = \Pr\big(F_{1,\,n-\mathrm{rank}} > F\big). \]

Family enters as a fixed categorical covariate (levels dropped to those present for that marker) — it plays the role of the structure term. This is ordinary least squares (OLS): there is no kinship/polygenic random effect \(K\). Chen 2019 instead used a mixed model y ~ Q(5 PCs) + SNP + K(IBS); the missing random \(K\) is what makes ours “not mixed”, so peak heights here are expected to run hotter — quantifying that inflation (\(\lambda_{GC}\), QQ) is a separate analysis.

The scan

scan1 <- function(m) {
  g <- geno[[m]]
  ok <- !is.na(g) & !is.na(y)
  if (sum(ok) < 20) {
    return(c(NA_real_, sum(ok)))
  }
  gg <- g[ok]
  if (stats::sd(gg) == 0) {
    return(c(NA_real_, sum(ok)))
  }
  yy <- y[ok]
  ff <- droplevels(fam[ok])
  Xr <- if (nlevels(ff) > 1) model.matrix(~ff) else matrix(1, sum(ok), 1)
  n <- sum(ok)
  RSS0 <- sum(lm.fit(Xr, yy)$residuals^2)
  fit <- lm.fit(cbind(Xr, gg), yy)
  RSS1 <- sum(fit$residuals^2)
  df2 <- n - fit$rank
  if (df2 <= 0 || RSS1 <= 0) {
    return(c(NA_real_, n))
  }
  c(pf(((RSS0 - RSS1) / 1) / (RSS1 / df2), 1, df2, lower.tail = FALSE), n)
}
out <- mclapply(mk, scan1, mc.cores = THREADS)
P <- vapply(out, `[`, numeric(1), 1)
N <- vapply(out, `[`, numeric(1), 2)

scan <- data.table(
  SNP = mk, CHR = as.integer(chr_by[mk]),
  BP = as.integer(pos_by[mk]), P = P,
  n = as.integer(N)
)[order(CHR, BP)]
fwrite(scan, here("data/teonam/stam_gwas_scan_unimputed.csv")) # canonical output

QC and results

fin <- scan[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
data.table(
  metric = c(
    "markers scanned", "markers tested (finite P)",
    "median lines / marker", "max -log10P",
    sprintf("genome-wide sig. (P < 1e-%d)", LOD)
  ),
  value = c(
    nrow(scan), nrow(fin), median(scan$n),
    round(max(-log10(fin$P)), 2), sum(fin$P < 10^(-LOD))
  )
)
                        metric    value
                        <char>    <num>
1:             markers scanned 51004.00
2:   markers tested (finite P) 51004.00
3:       median lines / marker   267.00
4:                 max -log10P    14.34
5: genome-wide sig. (P < 1e-5)  1334.00
top <- fin[order(P)][1:10, .(SNP, CHR, BP, n, logP = round(-log10(P), 2))]
knitr::kable(top, caption = "Top 10 STAM associations (unimputed scan)")
Top 10 STAM associations (unimputed scan)
SNP CHR BP n logP
S1_275704784 1 282394909 751 14.34
S1_252523134 1 258729363 748 13.70
S1_52347160 1 53041203 1236 13.57
S3_151334181 3 153463524 797 13.52
S1_272841586 1 279402734 530 13.52
S1_251137308 1 257311732 528 13.26
S3_152181419 3 154284127 797 13.18
S3_151940813 3 154069164 797 13.16
S3_151970380 3 154098728 797 13.15
S3_151971720 3 154100068 797 13.15 
ggplot(scan, aes(n)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 60, fill = "grey30") +
  labs(
    x = "lines tested per marker (families typing that marker)",
    y = "markers", title = "Per-marker sample size — the source of the natural scatter"
  )
# lightweight Manhattan (the annotated Fig-4C version lives in teonam-qtl-recovery)
m <- fin[order(CHR, BP)]
off <- m[, .(mx = max(BP)), by = CHR][order(CHR)]
off[, base := cumsum(shift(mx, fill = 0))]
m <- merge(m, off[, .(CHR, base)], by = "CHR")
m[, x := BP + base]
axis <- m[, .(mid = (min(x) + max(x)) / 2), by = CHR][order(CHR)]
ggplot(m, aes(x, -log10(P), colour = factor(CHR %% 2))) +
  geom_point(size = 0.5) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = LOD, linetype = "dotted") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("grey60", "black"), guide = "none") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = axis$mid, labels = axis$CHR, expand = c(0.01, 0)) +
  labs(
    x = "chromosome", y = expression(-log[10](italic(P))),
    title = "STAM unimputed GWAS (OLS: STAM ~ Family + marker)"
  )

Notes

  • This notebook is the canonical, tracked source for stam_gwas_scan_unimputed.csv; it supersedes the untracked agent/teonam_stam_gwas_unimputed.R (identical model, ported verbatim).
  • Model is OLS (a fixed-effects linear model) with no kinship term — deliberately faithful to the throwaway script, not to Chen’s MLM. The peak-height / \(\lambda_{GC}\) comparison against Chen’s Q + K mixed model is deferred to a dedicated inflation notebook. ```
